name: pypdf4

metadata.name: PyPDF4
classifiers: Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
A Pure-Python library built as a PDF toolkit.  It is capable of:

- extracting document information (title, author, ...)
- splitting documents page by page
- merging documents page by page
- cropping pages
- merging multiple pages into a single page
- encrypting and decrypting PDF files
- and more!

By being Pure-Python, it should run on any Python platform without any
dependencies on external libraries.  It can also work entirely on StringIO
objects rather than file streams, allowing for PDF manipulation in memory.
It is therefore a useful tool for websites that manage or manipulate PDFs.

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.isString
documentation: Test if arg is a string. Compatible with Python 2 and 3.
Make basic type tests more consistent

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.isInt
documentation: Test if arg is an int. Compatible with Python 2 and 3.

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.isBytes
documentation: Test if arg is a bytes instance. Compatible with Python 2 and 3.

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.formatWarning
documentation: custom implementation of warnings.formatwarning

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.readUntilWhitespace
documentation:
Reads non-whitespace characters and returns them.
Stops upon encountering whitespace or when maxchars is reached.

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.readNonWhitespace
documentation: Finds and reads the next non-whitespace character (ignores whitespace).

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.skipOverWhitespace
documentation:
Similar to readNonWhitespace, but returns a Boolean if more than
one whitespace character was read.

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.readUntilRegex
documentation:
Reads until the regular expression pattern matched (ignore the match)
Raise PdfStreamError on premature end-of-file.
:param bool ignore_eof: If true, ignore end-of-line and return immediately

functions.name: PyPDF4.utils.markLocation
documentation: Creates text file showing current location in context.

variables.name: PyPDF4.utils.bytes_type
documentation: Works the same in Python 2.X and 3.X

classes.name: PyPDF4.merger._MergedPage
documentation:
_MergedPage is used internally by PdfFileMerger to collect necessary
information on each page that is being merged.

classes.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.merge
documentation:
Merges the pages from the given file into the output file at the
specified page number.

```
param int position

The page number to insert this file. File will
be inserted after the given number.

param fileobj

A File Object or an object that supports the standard read
and seek methods similar to a File Object. Could also be a
string representing a path to a PDF file.

param str bookmark

Optionally, you may specify a bookmark to be applied at
the beginning of the included file by supplying the text of the bookmark.

param pages

can be a  or a (start, stop[, step]) tuple
to merge only the specified range of pages from the source
document into the output document.

param bool import_bookmarks

You may prevent the source document's bookmarks
from being imported by specifying this as False.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.append
documentation:
Identical to the {meth}`merge()<merge>` method, but assumes you want to concatenate
all pages onto the end of the file instead of specifying a position.

```
param fileobj

A File Object or an object that supports the standard read
and seek methods similar to a File Object. Could also be a
string representing a path to a PDF file.

param str bookmark

Optionally, you may specify a bookmark to be applied at
the beginning of the included file by supplying the text of the bookmark.

param pages

can be a  or a (start, stop[, step]) tuple
to merge only the specified range of pages from the source
document into the output document.

param bool import_bookmarks

You may prevent the source document's bookmarks
from being imported by specifying this as False.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.write
documentation:
Writes all data that has been merged to the given output file.

```
param fileobj

Output file. Can be a filename or any kind of
file-like object.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.close
documentation:
Shuts all file descriptors (input and output) and clears all memory
usage.

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.addMetadata
documentation:
Add custom metadata to the output.

:param dict infos: a Python dictionary where each key is a field
    and each value is your new metadata.
    Example: ``{u'/Title': u'My title'}``

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.setPageLayout
documentation:
Set the page layout

:param str layout: The page layout to be used

Valid layouts are:
     /NoLayout        Layout explicitly not specified
     /SinglePage      Show one page at a time
     /OneColumn       Show one column at a time
     /TwoColumnLeft   Show pages in two columns, odd-numbered pages on the left
     /TwoColumnRight  Show pages in two columns, odd-numbered pages on the right
     /TwoPageLeft     Show two pages at a time, odd-numbered pages on the left
     /TwoPageRight    Show two pages at a time, odd-numbered pages on the right

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.setPageMode
documentation:
Set the page mode.

:param str mode: The page mode to use.

Valid modes are:
    /UseNone         Do not show outlines or thumbnails panels
    /UseOutlines     Show outlines (aka bookmarks) panel
    /UseThumbs       Show page thumbnails panel
    /FullScreen      Fullscreen view
    /UseOC           Show Optional Content Group (OCG) panel
    /UseAttachments  Show attachments panel

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger._trim_dests
documentation:
Removes any named destinations that are not a part of the specified
page set.

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger._trim_outline
documentation:
Removes any outline/bookmark entries that are not a part of the
specified page set.

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.addBookmark
documentation:
Add a bookmark to this PDF file.

```
param str title

Title to use for this bookmark.

param int pagenum

Page number this bookmark will point to.

param parent

A reference to a parent bookmark to create nested
bookmarks.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.merger.PdfFileMerger.addNamedDestination
documentation:
Add a destination to the output.

:param str title: Title to use
:param int pagenum: Page number this destination points at.

Initializes a PdfFileMerger object. PdfFileMerger merges multiple PDFs
into a single PDF. It can concatenate, slice, insert, or any combination
of the above.

See the functions {meth}`merge()<merge>` (or {meth}`append()<append>`)
and {meth}`write()<write>` for usage information.

```
param bool strict

Determines whether user should be warned of all
problems and also causes some correctable problems to be fatal.
Defaults to True.
```

modules.name: PyPDF4.pagerange
documentation:
Representation and utils for ranges of PDF file pages.

Copyright (c) 2014, Steve Witham <switham_github@mac-guyver.com>.
All rights reserved. This software is available under a BSD license;
see https://github.com/claird/PyPDF4/blob/master/LICENSE
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.parse_filename_page_ranges
documentation:
Given a list of filenames and page ranges, return a list of
(filename, page_range) pairs.
First arg must be a filename; other ags are filenames, page-range
expressions, slice objects, or PageRange objects.
A filename not followed by a page range indicates all pages of the file.

variables.name: PyPDF4.pagerange._INT_RE
documentation: A decimal int, don't allow "-0".

variables.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PAGE_RANGE_ALL
documentation: The range of all pages.

classes.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.ParseError
documentation: groups:         12     34     5 6     7 8

classes.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange

methods.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange.__init__
documentation:
Initialize with either a slice -- giving the equivalent page range,
or a PageRange object -- making a copy,
or a string like
    "int", "[int]:[int]" or "[int]:[int]:[int]",
    where the brackets indicate optional ints.
{page_range_help}
Note the difference between this notation and arguments to slice():
    slice(3) means the first three pages;
    PageRange("3") means the range of only the fourth page.
    However PageRange(slice(3)) means the first three pages.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange.valid
documentation: True if input is a valid initializer for a PageRange.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange.to_slice
documentation: Return the slice equivalent of this page range.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange.__str__
documentation: A string like "1:2:3".

methods.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange.__repr__
documentation: A string like "PageRange('1:2:3')".

methods.name: PyPDF4.pagerange.PageRange.indices
documentation:
n is the length of the list of pages to choose from.
Returns arguments for range().  See help(slice.indices).

A slice-like representation of a range of page indices,
    i.e. page numbers, only starting at zero.
The syntax is like what you would put between brackets [ ].
The slice is one of the few Python types that can't be subclassed,
but this class converts to and from slices, and allows similar use.
  o  PageRange(str) parses a string representing a page range.
  o  PageRange(slice) directly "imports" a slice.
  o  to_slice() gives the equivalent slice.
  o  str() and repr() allow printing.
  o  indices(n) is like slice.indices(n).

classes.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_contributor
documentation:
Contributors to the resource (other than the authors). An unsorted
array of names.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_coverage
documentation: Text describing the extent or scope of the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_creator
documentation:
A sorted array of names of the authors of the resource, listed in order
of precedence.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_date
documentation:
A sorted array of dates (datetime.datetime instances) of signifigance to
the resource.  The dates and times are in UTC.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_description
documentation:
A language-keyed dictionary of textual descriptions of the content of the
resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_format
documentation: The mime-type of the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_identifier
documentation: Unique identifier of the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_language
documentation: An unordered array specifying the languages used in the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_publisher
documentation: An unordered array of publisher names.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_relation
documentation:
An unordered array of text descriptions of relationships to other
documents.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_rights
documentation:
A language-keyed dictionary of textual descriptions of the rights the
user has to this resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_source
documentation: Unique identifier of the work from which this resource was derived.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_subject
documentation:
An unordered array of descriptive phrases or keywrods that specify the
topic of the content of the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_title
documentation: A language-keyed dictionary of the title of the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.dc_type
documentation: An unordered array of textual descriptions of the document type.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.pdf_keywords
documentation: An unformatted text string representing document keywords.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.pdf_pdfversion
documentation: The PDF file version, for example 1.0, 1.3.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.pdf_producer
documentation: The name of the tool that created the PDF document.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.xmp_createDate
documentation:
The date and time the resource was originally created.  The date and
time are returned as a UTC datetime.datetime object.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.xmp_modifyDate
documentation:
The date and time the resource was last modified.  The date and time
are returned as a UTC datetime.datetime object.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.xmp_metadataDate
documentation:
The date and time that any metadata for this resource was last
changed.  The date and time are returned as a UTC datetime.datetime
object.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.xmp_creatorTool
documentation: The name of the first known tool used to create the resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.xmpmm_documentId
documentation: The common identifier for all versions and renditions of this resource.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.xmpmm_instanceId
documentation:
An identifier for a specific incarnation of a document, updated each
time a file is saved.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.xmp.XmpInformation.custom_properties
documentation:
Retrieves custom metadata properties defined in the undocumented pdfx
metadata schema.

```
return

a dictionary of key/value items for custom metadata properties.

rtype

dict
```

An object that represents Adobe XMP metadata.
Usually accessed by {meth}`getXmpMetadata()<PyPDF4.PdfFileReader.getXmpMetadata>`

functions.name: PyPDF4.generic.createStringObject
documentation: Given a string (either a "str" or "unicode"), create a ByteStringObject or a
TextStringObject to represent the string.

methods.name: PyPDF4.generic.PdfObject.getObject
documentation: Resolves indirect references.

classes.name: PyPDF4.generic.ByteStringObject
documentation: Represents a string object where the text encoding could not be determined.
This occurs quite often, as the PDF spec doesn't provide an alternate way to
represent strings -- for example, the encryption data stored in files (like
/O) is clearly not text, but is still stored in a "String" object.

classes.name: PyPDF4.generic.TextStringObject
documentation: Represents a string object that has been decoded into a real unicode string.
If read from a PDF document, this string appeared to match the
PDFDocEncoding, or contained a UTF-16BE BOM mark to cause UTF-16 decoding to
occur.

methods.name: PyPDF4.generic.DictionaryObject.getXmpMetadata
documentation: Retrieves XMP (Extensible Metadata Platform) data relevant to the
this object, if available.
Stability: Added in v1.12, will exist for all future v1.x releases.
@return Returns a {@link #xmp.XmpInformation XmlInformation} instance
that can be used to access XMP metadata from the document.  Can also
return None if no metadata was found on the document root.

classes.name: PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject.lowerLeft
documentation:
Property to read and modify the lower left coordinate of this box
in (x,y) form.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject.lowerRight
documentation:
Property to read and modify the lower right coordinate of this box
in (x,y) form.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject.upperLeft
documentation:
Property to read and modify the upper left coordinate of this box
in (x,y) form.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject.upperRight
documentation:
Property to read and modify the upper right coordinate of this box
in (x,y) form.

This class is used to represent *page boxes* in PyPDF4. These boxes include:

> - {attr}`artBox <PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.artBox>`
> - {attr}`bleedBox <PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.bleedBox>`
> - {attr}`cropBox <PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.cropBox>`
> - {attr}`mediaBox <PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.mediaBox>`
> - {attr}`trimBox <PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.trimBox>`

classes.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.fieldType
documentation: Read-only property accessing the type of this field.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.parent
documentation: Read-only property accessing the parent of this field.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.kids
documentation: Read-only property accessing the kids of this field.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.name
documentation: Read-only property accessing the name of this field.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.altName
documentation: Read-only property accessing the alternate name of this field.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.mappingName
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the mapping name of this field. This
name is used by PyPDF4 as a key in the dictionary returned by
{meth}`getFields()<PyPDF4.PdfFileReader.getFields>`

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.flags
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the field flags, specifying various
characteristics of the field (see Table 8.70 of the PDF 1.7 reference).

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.value
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the value of this field. Format
varies based on field type.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.defaultValue
documentation: Read-only property accessing the default value of this field.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Field.additionalActions
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the additional actions dictionary.
This dictionary defines the field's behavior in response to trigger events.
See Section 8.5.2 of the PDF 1.7 reference.

A class representing a field dictionary. This class is accessed through
{meth}`getFields()<PyPDF4.PdfFileReader.getFields>`

classes.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.title
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the destination title.

```
rtype

str
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.page
documentation:

```
rtype

int
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.typ
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the destination type.

```
rtype

str
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.zoom
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the zoom factor.

```
rtype

int, or None if not available.
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.left
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the left horizontal coordinate.

```
rtype

int, or None if not available.
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.right
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the right horizontal coordinate.

```
rtype

int, or None if not available.
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.top
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the top vertical coordinate.

```
rtype

int, or None if not available.
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.generic.Destination.bottom
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the bottom vertical coordinate.

```
rtype

int, or None if not available.
```

A class representing a destination within a PDF file.
See section 8.2.1 of the PDF 1.6 reference.

```
param str title

Title of this destination.

param int page

Page number of this destination.

param str typ

How the destination is displayed.

param args

Additional arguments may be necessary depending on the type.

raises PdfReadError

If destination type is invalid.
```

Valid `typ` arguments (see PDF spec for details):

: /Fit

  No additional arguments

  /XYZ

  [left] [top] [zoomFactor]

  /FitH

  [top]

  /FitV

  [left]

  /FitR

  [left] [bottom] [right] [top]

  /FitB

  No additional arguments

  /FitBH

  [top]

  /FitBV

  [left]

classes.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addPage
documentation:
Adds a page to this PDF file. The page is usually acquired from a
{class}`PdfFileReader<PdfFileReader>` instance.

The page to add to the document. Should be

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addBlankPage

Appends a blank page to this PDF file and returns it. If no page size

space units.

if width and height are not defined

Inserts a blank page to this PDF file and returns it. If no page size

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addJS
documentation:
Add Javascript which will launch upon opening this PDF.

```
param str javascript

Your Javascript.
```

```pycon
>>> output.addJS("this.print({bUI:true,bSilent:false,bShrinkToFit:true});")
# Example: This will launch the print window when the PDF is opened.
```

Embed a file inside the PDF.

:param str fdata: The data in the file.

https://www.adobe.com/content/dam/Adobe/en/devnet/acrobat/pdfs/PDF32000_2008.pdf
Section 7.11.3

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.appendPagesFromReader
documentation:
Copy pages from reader to writer. Includes an optional callback parameter
which is invoked after pages are appended to the writer.

```
param reader

a PdfFileReader object from which to copy page
annotations to this writer object.  The writer's annots
```

will then be updated
\:callback after_page_append (function): Callback function that is invoked after

> each page is appended to the writer. Callback signature:
>
> ```
> param writer_pageref (PDF page reference)
>
> Reference to the page
> appended to the writer.
> ```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.updatePageFormFieldValues
documentation:
Update the form field values for a given page from a fields dictionary.
Copy field texts and values from fields to page.

```

Page reference from PDF writer where the annotations
and field data will be updated.

param fields

a Python dictionary of field names (/T) and text
values (/V)
```

PdfFileReader from the document root should be copied.
```

\:callback after_page_append

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.cloneDocumentFromReader
documentation:
Create a copy (clone) of a document from a PDF file reader

```
param reader

PDF file reader instance from which the clone
should be created.

callback after_page_append (function)

Callback function that is invoked after
each page is appended to the writer. Signature includes a reference to the
appended page (delegates to appendPagesFromReader). Callback signature:

param writer_pageref (PDF page reference)

Reference to the page just
appended to the document.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.encrypt
documentation:
Encrypt this PDF file with the PDF Standard encryption handler.

:param str user_pwd: The "user password", which allows for opening
    and reading the PDF file with the restrictions provided.
:param str owner_pwd: The "owner password", which allows for
    opening the PDF files without any restrictions.  By default,
    the owner password is the same as the user password.
:param bool use_128bit: flag as to whether to use 128bit
    encryption.  When false, 40bit encryption will be used.  By default,
    this flag is on.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.write
documentation:
Writes the collection of pages added to this object out as a PDF file.

```
param stream

An object to write the file to.  The object must support
the write method and the tell method, similar to a file object.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addMetadata
documentation:
Add custom metadata to the output.

:param dict infos: a Python dictionary where each key is a field
    and each value is your new metadata.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addBookmark

Add a bookmark to this PDF file.

param str title

Title to use for this bookmark.

param int pagenum

Page number this bookmark will point to.

param parent

A reference to a parent bookmark to create nested
bookmarks.

param tuple color

Color of the bookmark as a red, green, blue tuple
from 0.0 to 1.0

param bool bold

Bookmark is bold

param bool italic

Bookmark is italic

param str fit

The fit of the destination page. See
 for details.
```

documentation: Removes links and annotations from this output.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addURI
documentation:
Add an URI from a rectangular area to the specified page.
This uses the basic structure of AddLink

```
param int pagenum

index of the page on which to place the URI action.

param int uri

string -- uri of resource to link to.

param rect

 or array of four
integers specifying the clickable rectangular area
[xLL, yLL, xUR, yUR], or string in the form "[ xLL yLL xUR yUR ]".

param border

if provided, an array describing border-drawing
properties. See the PDF spec for details. No border will be
drawn if this argument is omitted.
```

REMOVED FIT/ZOOM ARG
-John Mulligan

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.addLink
documentation:
Add an internal link from a rectangular area to the specified page.

```
param int pagenum

index of the page on which to place the link.

param int pagedest

index of the page to which the link should go.

param rect

 or array of four
integers specifying the clickable rectangular area
[xLL, yLL, xUR, yUR], or string in the form "[ xLL yLL xUR yUR ]".

param border

if provided, an array describing border-drawing
properties. See the PDF spec for details. No border will be
drawn if this argument is omitted.

param str fit

Page fit or 'zoom' option (see below). Additional arguments may need
to be supplied. Passing None will be read as a null value for that coordinate.
```

Valid zoom arguments (see Table 8.2 of the PDF 1.7 reference for details):

: /Fit

  No additional arguments

  /XYZ

  [left] [top] [zoomFactor]

  /FitH

  [top]

  /FitV

  [left]

  /FitR

  [left] [bottom] [right] [top]

  /FitB

  No additional arguments

  /FitBH

  [top]

  /FitBV

  [left]

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.getPageLayout
documentation:
Get the page layout.
See {meth}`setPageLayout()<PdfFileWriter.setPageLayout>` for a description of valid layouts.

```
return

Page layout currently being used.

rtype

str, None if not specified
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.setPageLayout
documentation:
Set the page layout

:param str layout: The page layout to be used

Valid layouts are:
     /NoLayout        Layout explicitly not specified
     /SinglePage      Show one page at a time
     /OneColumn       Show one column at a time
     /TwoColumnLeft   Show pages in two columns, odd-numbered pages on the left
     /TwoColumnRight  Show pages in two columns, odd-numbered pages on the right
     /TwoPageLeft     Show two pages at a time, odd-numbered pages on the left
     /TwoPageRight    Show two pages at a time, odd-numbered pages on the right

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.getPageMode
documentation:
Get the page mode.
See {meth}`setPageMode()<PdfFileWriter.setPageMode>` for a description
of valid modes.

```
return

Page mode currently being used.

rtype

str, None if not specified
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileWriter.setPageMode
documentation:
Set the page mode.

:param str mode: The page mode to use.

Valid modes are:
    /UseNone         Do not show outlines or thumbnails panels
    /UseOutlines     Show outlines (aka bookmarks) panel
    /UseThumbs       Show page thumbnails panel
    /FullScreen      Fullscreen view
    /UseOC           Show Optional Content Group (OCG) panel
    /UseAttachments  Show attachments panel

Read and write property accessing the {meth}`getPageMode()<PdfFileWriter.getPageMode>`
and {meth}`setPageMode()<PdfFileWriter.setPageMode>` methods.

This class supports writing PDF files out, given pages produced by another
class (typically {class}`PdfFileReader<PdfFileReader>`).

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileReader.getFields
documentation:
Extracts field data if this PDF contains interactive form fields.
The *tree* and *retval* parameters are for recursive use.

```
param fileobj

A file object (usually a text file) to write
a report to on all interactive form fields found.

return

A dictionary where each key is a field name, and each
value is a  object. By
default, the mapping name is used for keys.

rtype

dict, or None if form data could not be located.
```
A select group of relevant field attributes. For the complete list,
see section 8.6.2 of the PDF 1.7 reference.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileReader.decrypt
documentation:
When using an encrypted / secured PDF file with the PDF Standard
encryption handler, this function will allow the file to be decrypted.
It checks the given password against the document's user password and
owner password, and then stores the resulting decryption key if either
password is correct.

It does not matter which password was matched. Both passwords provide
the correct decryption key that will allow the document to be used with
this library.

```
param str password

The password to match.

return

0 if the password failed, 1 if the password matched the user
password, and 2 if the password matched the owner password.

rtype

int

raises NotImplementedError

if document uses an unsupported encryption
method.
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PdfFileReader.isEncrypted
documentation:
Read-only boolean property showing whether this PDF file is encrypted.
Note that this property, if true, will remain true even after the
{meth}`decrypt()<PdfFileReader.decrypt>` method is called.

Initializes a PdfFileReader object. This operation can take some time, as
the PDF stream's cross-reference tables are read into memory.

```
param stream

A File object or an object that supports the standard read
and seek methods similar to a File object. Could also be a
string representing a path to a PDF file.

param bool strict

Determines whether user should be warned of all
problems and also causes some correctable problems to be fatal.
Defaults to True.

param warndest

Destination for logging warnings (defaults to
sys.stderr).

param bool overwriteWarnings

Determines whether to override Python's
warnings.py module with a custom implementation (defaults to
True).
```

Returns a new blank page.
If `width` or `height` is `None`, try to get the page size
from the last page of *pdf*.

param pdf

PDF file the page belongs to

The width of the new page expressed in default user
space units.

param float height

The height of the new page expressed in default user
space units.

return

the new blank page:

rtype



raises PageSizeNotDefinedError

if pdf is None or contains
no page
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.rotateClockwise
documentation:
Rotates a page clockwise by increments of 90 degrees.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.rotateCounterClockwise

Rotates a page counter-clockwise by increments of 90 degrees.

documentation:
Accesses the page contents.

```
return

the /Contents object, or None if it doesn't exist.
/Contents is optional, as described in PDF Reference  7.7.3.3
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.mergePage
documentation:
Merges the content streams of two pages into one. Resource references
(i.e. fonts) are maintained from both pages. The mediabox/cropbox/etc
of this page are not altered. The parameter page's content stream will
be added to the end of this page's content stream, meaning that it will
be drawn after, or "on top" of this page.

```
param PageObject page2

The page to be merged into this one. Should be
an instance of .
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.mergeTransformedPage
documentation:
This is similar to mergePage, but a transformation matrix is
applied to the merged stream.

```
param PageObject page2

The page to be merged into this one. Should be
an instance of .

param tuple ctm

a 6-element tuple containing the operands of the
transformation matrix

param bool expand

Whether the page should be expanded to fit the dimensions
of the page to be merged.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.mergeScaledPage
documentation:
This is similar to mergePage, but the stream to be merged is scaled
by appling a transformation matrix.

```
param PageObject page2

The page to be merged into this one. Should be
an instance of .

param float scale

The scaling factor

param bool expand

Whether the page should be expanded to fit the
dimensions of the page to be merged.
```

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.mergeRotatedPage
documentation:

the page to be merged into this one. Should be

param bool expand

Whether the page should be expanded to fit the
dimensions of the page to be merged.
```

Applies a transformation matrix to the page.

    transformation matrix.
Applys a transformation matrix the page.
@param ctm   A 6 elements tuple containing the operands of the
transformation matrix

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.scale
documentation:
Scales a page by the given factors by appling a transformation
matrix to its content and updating the page size.

:param float sx: The scaling factor on horizontal axis.
:param float sy: The scaling factor on vertical axis.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.scaleBy
documentation:
Scales a page by the given factor by appling a transformation
matrix to its content and updating the page size.

:param float factor: The scaling factor (for both X and Y axis).

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.scaleTo
documentation:
Scales a page to the specified dimentions by appling a
transformation matrix to its content and updating the page size.

:param float width: The new width.
:param float height: The new heigth.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.compressContentStreams
documentation:
Compresses the size of this page by joining all content streams and
applying a FlateDecode filter.

However, it is possible that this function will perform no action if
content stream compression becomes "automatic" for some reason.

methods.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.extractText
documentation:
Locate all text drawing commands, in the order they are provided in the
content stream, and extract the text. This works well for some PDF
files, but poorly for others, depending on the generator used. This will
be refined in the future. Do not rely on the order of text coming out of
this function, as it will change if this function is made more
sophisticated.

```
return

a unicode string object.
```

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.mediaBox
documentation:
A {class}`RectangleObject<PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject>`, expressed in default user space units,
defining the boundaries of the physical medium on which the page is
intended to be displayed or printed.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.cropBox
documentation:
A {class}`RectangleObject<PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject>`, expressed in default user space units,
defining the visible region of default user space. When the page is
displayed or printed, its contents are to be clipped (cropped) to this
rectangle and then imposed on the output medium in some
implementation-defined manner. Default value: same as {attr}`mediaBox<mediaBox>`.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.bleedBox
documentation:
A {class}`RectangleObject<PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject>`, expressed in default user space units,
defining the region to which the contents of the page should be clipped
when output in a production enviroment.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.trimBox
documentation:
A {class}`RectangleObject<PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject>`, expressed in default user space units,
defining the intended dimensions of the finished page after trimming.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.PageObject.artBox
documentation:
A {class}`RectangleObject<PyPDF4.generic.RectangleObject>`, expressed in default user space units,
defining the extent of the page's meaningful content as intended by the
page's creator.

This class represents a single page within a PDF file. Typically this
object will be created by accessing the
{meth}`getPage()<PyPDF4.PdfFileReader.getPage>` method of the
{class}`PdfFileReader<PyPDF4.PdfFileReader>` class, but it is
also possible to create an empty page with the
{meth}`createBlankPage()<PageObject.createBlankPage>` static method.

```
param pdf

PDF file the page belongs to.

param indirectRef

Stores the original indirect reference to
this object in its source PDF
```

if the subject is not specified.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.DocumentInformation.subject_raw
documentation: The "raw" version of subject; can return a `ByteStringObject`.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.DocumentInformation.creator
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the document's **creator**. If the
document was converted to PDF from another format, this is the name of the
application (e.g. OpenOffice) that created the original document from
which it was converted. Returns a unicode string (`TextStringObject`)
or `None` if the creator is not specified.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.DocumentInformation.creator_raw
documentation: The "raw" version of creator; can return a `ByteStringObject`.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.DocumentInformation.producer
documentation:
Read-only property accessing the document's **producer**.
If the document was converted to PDF from another format, this is
the name of the application (for example, OSX Quartz) that converted
it to PDF. Returns a unicode string (`TextStringObject`)
or `None` if the producer is not specified.

class_variables.name: PyPDF4.pdf.DocumentInformation.producer_raw
documentation: The "raw" version of producer; can return a `ByteStringObject`.

A class representing the basic document metadata provided in a PDF File.
This class is accessible through
{meth}`getDocumentInfo()<PyPDF4.PdfFileReader.getDocumentInfo()>`

All text properties of the document metadata have
*two* properties, eg. author and author_raw. The non-raw property will
always return a `TextStringObject`, making it ideal for a case where
the metadata is being displayed. The raw property can sometimes return
a `ByteStringObject`, if PyPDF4 was unable to decode the string's
text encoding; this requires additional safety in the caller and
therefore is not as commonly accessed.
